summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a scorching July 1990 Three Rivers show highlighted by a ripping Terrapin>Drums>Space>Miracle sequence.
review: The recording, taken from the first View From The Vault video release, delivers a clean sound‑board mix that lets listeners hear the heat of the night and the crowd’s excitement. Musically the concert is a fan favorite – the Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Miracle stretch and an impassioned Estimated Prophet stand out as peak moments. Overall the show feels legendary and the audio quality matches that reputation.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed, high‑star reviews praise both performance (standout jams) and the video‑derived soundboard source; no recording complaints were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Taken from the View From The Vault video, the mix is clear and well‑balanced; reviewers note no audio flaws and appreciate the captured crowd ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert builds a hot, high‑energy vibe from the opening CSN cover through a fiery second set; transitions (especially Terrapin > Drums > Space > Miracle) are praised for their seamless flow.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir repeatedly rushed the front of the stage during Estimated Prophet, energizing the crowd.
